messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Just a heads-up – I’ve been tinkering with something called MeshRank It’s basically a way of giving a bit more visibility into what the mesh is actually doing, without changing how MeshCORE works. No internet routing, no messing with RF, nothing in the message path. It just listens, observes, and feeds back what already happened. Why I built it: • New repeaters often get switched off because it’s hard to tell if they’re helping • People end up spamming test messages just to see if anything’s alive • When stuff doesn’t propagate, there’s no clue why MeshRank gives things like delivery confidence, shows which repeaters are contributing, and highlights where messages are being seen but not going any further. Observers are totally passive — they don’t transmit or interfere in any way. If MeshRank fell over tomorrow, the mesh would carry on exactly the same. I’m looking for a few folks (UK first) who wouldn’t mind running an observer and helping build some early coverage — high ground / urban spots especially useful. If you fancy a look: https://meshrank.net Happy to answer questions or take criticism

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: The meshrank.net site is starting to pull decent stats I’m short of an observer in this area. If anyone is willing to help out let me know. I have some spare boards. I can get one posted out. Just email me your details to Stu.stoke@gmail.com

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Pushed a few new bits to meshrank.net tonight: • Personalised channels • You can now add channels and see what’s popular • More progress on Repeater Rank • Added low-scoring neighbours (the ones the app effectively ignores) — handy for seeing how far your signals are actually travelling • Next up: more work on live mesh stats
messages.content_html: Pushed a few new bits to meshrank.net tonight: • Personalised channels • You can now add channels and see what’s popular • More progress on Repeater Rank • Added low-scoring neighbours (the ones the app effectively ignores) — handy for seeing how far your signals are actually travelling • Next up: more work on live mesh stats

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: Morning all — a few updates to MeshRank.net: Heltec V4 can now run as a combined Observer + Repeater (beta). An NTP time sync has been added once Wi-Fi connects, so the device always has the correct date/time after reboot. This also includes the Heltec V4 SX1262 / GC1109 RX fix. As this is beta, please don’t update the repeater that’s bolted to your roof just yet The MQTT feed is now open for anyone already using meshcoretomqtt (or similar) and wanting to mirror their packet stream into MeshRank. Advanced packet routing maps are nearly finished. These are based on mutual neighbour connections and can infer likely paths where there are gaps in the observed route. I’m also finalising MeshLive — a real-time visual view of the mesh, including country-wide noise floor, live routes, and packets per minute. This will be released to observers first.

messages.author: S7uart G5AIL
messages.text: You requested a live map showing last 60 minutes of all packet data overlaid on a map showing the full mesh interconnection. No problem it’s live now on MeshRank.net under the MeshRank page. Enjoy
